latestCycle.goal: trim thetaeCoeff inside the current convection package to recover the 30-day dry-belt ratio without giving back the 90-day gain
latestCycle.verifiedImprovement: promoting vertParams.thetaeCoeff from 11 to 10.5 improved the tracked-code north subtropical dry-belt ratio from 1.094 to 1.029 at 30 days and from 1.031 to 1.015 at 90 days while keeping the same single blocker and preserving the broad circulation, storm-track, cloud-balance, and stability categories

blockingGaps: northern subtropical dry-belt moisture partitioning still fails in both fresh quick and seasonal audits, though the gap is now smaller at 1.029 at 30 days and 1.015 at 90 days
live browser realism and runtime smoothness still need a fresh run on the thetaeCoeff=10.5 baseline before this improved moisture package can be considered signed off
the lower-right white panel plus the remaining earth-update and wind-target issues from the last live run are still unresolved until a fresh browser-backed run proves otherwise
annual / 365-day evidence is still missing for any long-horizon or world-class claim
world-class status still requires realism and smoothness to pass in the same fresh live run
cycleInputs: latest runtime summary from npm run agent:summarize-runtime-log

seasonalDurabilityRecovery.tradeoffNote: Lowering thetaeCoeff to 10.5 recovers the 30-day dry-belt ratio and slightly improves the 90-day ratio as well, so the quick-versus-seasonal tradeoff is now materially smaller than before.

runtimeTextureWarningFix.result: fresh reopened localhost tab no longer reproduced the startup THREE.WebGLRenderer texture-update warning

browserObservation.note: The last live run predates the new thetaeCoeff=10.5 baseline. It already proved the startup texture-warning spam was fixed, but it still showed a lower-right white panel and runtime warnings. A fresh browser-backed rerun is now required on the improved moisture baseline.

defaultNextPriority: run a browser-backed live verification cycle on the thetaeCoeff=10.5 baseline before another headless-only tuning cycle
if the live run confirms the improved baseline, keep the next physics cycle on northern subtropical dry-belt moisture partitioning and ITCZ-adjacent hydrology

preserve the thetaeCoeff=10.5 moisture package and the texture-readiness guard while investigating the lower-right white panel or runtime issues only if they still block signoff after the new live run
run the annual planetary audit after the dry-belt fix holds through another seasonal follow-through
keep validation on the clean world-class checkout only
